reviews.review_text: when someone tells you “trust me bro, this gets good 300 hours in”, i PROMISE you this applies to this game. but you gotta love reading. there's lot of it. but the payoff is worth it. don't listen to the tourists who tell you to skip any part of the MSQ.

the game’s fun, but the client is diabolical

i’d rather work a 20-hr shift than go through the process of trying to make it work again
Registration and early game are awful. As recurring player i also cant just join the game as a trial, i have to purchase a sub

FF14 is maybe the most egregious example of "it gets really good after x amount of hours" I can think of, because it genuinely does get fun and interesting after you finish the base A Realm Reborn content, but that is easily about 70 hours of gameplay if you speedrun it. There are little bits of good content interspersed throughout that, and they've been doing their best to streamline it more and more over the years, but it is still a massive time investment that I don't think I would have bothered with if I didn't have friends already in the game. I think it's a testament to how immediately the game changes pace and upends the story so far as it moves into Heavensward that the game is still remembered fondly at all, because for most of the base MSQ I don't think most people will get it.

With that said, I'm far from deep enough into the game as an MMO to speak on it in that regard, and it may be another 300 hours before I ever reach that point. What I will say is that as someone who was mostly in it for the promise of later expansions' interesting stories when I started it, I appreciate the ways they've been making it more approachable as a solo player, but I also have yet to have a bad time in the moments where it does start making you group up for things. The community is almost suspiciously nice and welcoming, sometimes a little overbearingly so. I think in some ways the game stops short of feeling like the untamed wild west feeling you used to get from early MMOs long ago, in favor of being more streamlined and coherent, but it captures the social feeling of those games far better than what I've experienced in other modern MMOs. 

There's a bit of absurdity in recommending FF14 at all, though, because yes, 70 hours is an insane amount of time to ask as an up-front investment before you get to the truly good parts. But in the same way, that initial 70 hours feels so small when I look at my current 260 hours, being not even out of the post-game of Heavensward yet, and even more insignificant when I see the thousands of hours my friends have put into it. And after those 260 hours, I am honestly excited to get back into it and pick up where I left off in the story. Even if you're just a singleplayer Final Fantasy fan without much interest in the MMO aspect, I think there's enough great writing and characters here for it to be worth at least trying, and once you're in it you may find that you like the online element more than you thought you would anyways. As the memes famously say, you can play for FREE up to level whatever and play through several expansions' worth of content, so why not?
Turns out, you have to pay a monthly fee to even play after the first 30 days on TOP of buying the full game already.

Who in their right mind puts the install of the game behind the launchers login? Steam says I've played this game for 4.5 hours. I've never been able to get to the character creation screen. 
It keeps crashing during the install and who the hell makes a launcher that doesn't allow you to stay signed in? I don't even know if I would enjoy this game.
If you ever decide to take a break from the game, good luck trying to log into your Square Enix account

I really wanted to try and like this game but it's not fun, there isn't a point in killing anything, it doesn't matter what you kill you pretty much don't get any reward for fighting mobs or anything so there is no incentive to even fight, you don't get loot or coins that way and you barely get any exp.

the game isn't for someone that wants to fight and get good gear and it doesn't matter what gear you have because of the world balance system, everyone are basically mirror copies of each other (stat wise) every one have the same skills and stats so it defeats the purpose of well everything.

you basically just run from quest to quest and get gold that way then you have to buy items from the markets which everyone has access to so it makes your character feel pointless because you will never have unique gear.

now if you want a game where everyone is just standing around dancing and doing emotes this sure the game is perfect for you but if you want that having to work to make your character strong and unique then this is a pass.

graphics are good but gamplay is so boring.

This game isn't worth playing anymore since I can't seem to play it anyway. wish I could just pick-up where I left off...but no. I want a refund!!!
Sometimes I turn on my PC to do something and my muscle memory just opens this game up and I forget everything I had to do...

To even have the privilege of playing the game YOU paid for you have to invest a good 2-3 hours just making your account work through the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ 3rd party website they force you to use. If you want to change anything or have any problems (which you most certainly will) you have to contact customer support and wait a few days to get in contact with a human. Until they make a capable launcher, I can't recommend it. But lets be real, Square Enix would never.

I can't recommend this game until I can get a working launcher. I haven't been able to get anything but a logo on the launcher. What can you do when it doesn't show anything but a logo? Yes, I reinstalled and tried all of the basic things with the same results and yes, I more than exceed the recommended specs for it. It just won't load anything beyond the launcher. Final Fantasy XIV Online - Complete Edition

Honestly a great game. There is so much to do in this game. The number of quests and length of the story if you're just starting out will feel overwhelming. I know I have personally taken a couple breaks during my time playing just so I didn't get burnt out. The earlier game was a bit boring to me personally, but once you're going and past the initial bit, it doesn't take too long to pull you into the story. I am currently starting Dawntrail and have played through the entire story without skipping anything. The main story sections have been a lot of fun, and a good portion of the battle music is amazing. The community is great and there is usually someone who will help you out if you need it. Overall, I'd highly recommend you try this out if you like Final Fantasy games.

Feel like I got scammed, game took 14 hours to download and it periodically kicked me out of the download, when I finally opened it it literally wouldn’t work. I just got a black screen and it would crash. On top of that, since the launcher took 14 hours to download no I get no refund. Buy the game on the official site don’t use steam.

At this point, it takes around 500 hours to catch up to current content, and that is only going to increase with the introduction of the new expansion. All that time as well is almost exclusively just talking to npcs one after the other with very minimal content around that. Its like playing a visual novel really.

Once you get to Heavensward. it's where it become very good ! Insane mmorpg in my opinion !

Unfortunately, not entirely clear/obvious when buying through steam, being the end of a 3rd dot point of 'importan fine text'
THIS GAME REQUIRES A SUBSCRIPTION after buying the base game. 
plus the whole square enix system is very silly. 

however for the two hours of game I actually played, the game seems fun, very involved and could spend a lot of time in it, but i am not paying $150 a year for the privellege

Finally gave the game a decent try with the trial (not this account). Just don't like the game. 
The writing is not great. The story is tedious and drawn out, same for the travelling between quests.
Overbearing tutorials.

Its one of the bigger MMO's that just couldn't grab me, I felt like I was forcing myself to the next goal: unlocking airship travel, unlocking mount,... wasn't enjoying it after level 30 MSQ so better to quit and play something else.

You usually hear people say: "It gets better after ARR"... well the game drives me to quit before I reach the first expansion so that's no good.

Verry much has its appeal and strong suits. It has a robust raiding experience which is neato and fun, there is some other fun bits here and there. But the main problem is it feels like its all balls no shaft, like 90% of the content is balls then you get two inches of shaft. I am aware that there are long and 'rewarding' grinds like relic weapons and the eureka/bozja/occult areas. There's heaven on high and the other towers/holes for more grinds which consist of mashing your keyboard in an attempt to not fall asleep. Which is not to say that the game is all bad theres a good 8 inches of shaft but a much more unfortunate 80 inches of balls. The story can feel like a slog esp when it takes a whole expansion's worth of story to FEEL like there's something at stake. And the physical gameplay is a slightly interactive VN, 'The NOexistenceN of you AND me' really spoiled me on how interactive a Visual novel can be. There is interaction its just that other than a handful of choice words you can say which don't have any bearing on the outcome of the story, All the interaction comes from combat which until about 3 expansions in there are VERY few options on how to play an encounter or boss. Jobs/Classes feel like a slightly different flavor of the last one with each having a 120 second, 60 second, and 30 second burst timer with each one layering on top of on another making a very fun 20 seconds when the two minute comes around and then a snoozefest for the other 100 seconds. All that said I don't feel like FFXIV is bad, It has a great story with interesting characters, job design is cool and flashy with few loose ends and feels whole, the soundtrack is made by Soken who has me soaking. But the game does not feel like it wants you to play it. It does not feel like it wants to grab you alure you in with gobsmacking features or a brilliant new player experience. I would not Recommend to someone who wants to feel it out and see if they like it. I would recommend this to someone who loves 80 inches of balls for 8 inches of shaft.

Play this game for FREE until level 70.  DO NOT BUY THE GAME UNTIL YOU CAN NOT PLAY FOR FREE ANYMORE!!!!!  There is over 1200 hours of gameplay (according to what I read, only after buying the game) that you can play for FREE.  Once you buy the game, you have 30 days before you have to start paying for a SUBSCRIPTION to play the game.   I bought the full game for $60 on Steam and still have to purchase a $12-20 subscription to play the game after the 30 day mark.  And you can't go back to the free version even if you ask for a refund and try to go back.

I also love that you don't have to make multiple characters for multiple classes. Definitely more fun with friends. If you're thinking about it or on the fence, start the free trial!

As a long-time MMORPG player who spent years in World of Warcraft, I came to Final Fantasy XIV during the Shadowlands era of WoW. At the time, I was growing increasingly frustrated with the direction Blizzard was taking the game, and I wanted to see what all the hype surrounding FFXIV was about.

Nearly 400 hours later, I can confidently say that FFXIV rekindled my love for MMORPGs.

What immediately stood out was the emphasis on narrative. Unlike WoW, where the story often feels secondary to the gameplay loop, FFXIV treats its story as the foundation of the entire experience. Your character feels like an active participant in a grand adventure, and progression feels meaningful rather than simply rushing toward the next endgame treadmill.

The job system is another major highlight. Every job feels distinct and polished, and being able to level every class on a single character is one of those features that feels so obvious that it's hard to go back to games that don't offer it.

The cities and hubs are beautifully designed and genuinely feel alive. Whether I'm questing, crafting, gathering, or simply exploring, I constantly encounter other players. The world feels populated and social in a way many modern MMOs struggle to achieve.

Crafting and gathering are also far more engaging than I expected. They're full-fledged progression systems with their own identities, and they contribute to a player-driven economy that remains relevant throughout the game.

While no MMO is perfect, FFXIV succeeded where many others failed: it made me excited to log in again. After years of watching WoW become increasingly stale and stagnant, FFXIV reminded me why I fell in love with the MMORPG genre in the first place.

If you're a former WoW player who feels burned out, especially if you've been around since the Shadowlands era, I strongly recommend giving Final Fantasy XIV a chance.
i have amost 100 hours and im not even lvl 60 but ngl this games story is so good i would sell my organs to play more plus the dungons and the charactor creation this game is worth the subscription
Totally the best co-op game! I just don't like that you have to subscribe monthly to keep your character if you are playing.
One of the best games I've ever played. If you're able to push through ARR (which is decent), the expansions are really good. Shadowbringers is my personal favorite.

Awesome MMO, fun story and the classes ("Jobs") play well. Just finished the Heavensward campaign and it was a blast! As a WoW transplant, I'm having a great time with FF14. Perhaps most important, the community is amazing. In 115 hours, I haven't ran into a single toxic person. Everyone is helpful, even when I mess up. Rare to see in an online game these days.
so good. so, SO good. genuinely one of my favorite stories of all damn time and the best MMO on the market imho
I love this game and everything about it ! ive been playing for 11 years now and its never been better! cant wait the conutined updates and content

Pretty much the only fun MMO left at the moment. I have about 300 hours total (200 on free trial) so far and while the game can feel like a bit of a drag at times, it always ends up redeeming itself soon after. It should also be noted, this game is not for everyone. It's an extremely slow burn MMO unlike most of the MMOs that we see get pushed out these days that let you reach endgame in just a few hours. (Also the combat is genuinely the most boring thing in the world until you get to like level 50 which is when every class finally starts to open up imo)

 I also hate to be one of those people that goes "The game gets good after x amount of hours", but that's unfortunately just the reality of this game. A Realm Reborn (the base game) is the longest part of the entire story and is also unfortunately the most boring and can't really be skipped if you want to pay any attention the story later on when it gets good. Once you get to Heavensward (the first expansion), the game immediately gets 10x better. I'd personally recommend everybody gives the free trial a shot at least given you get access to nearly everything up through the first 3 expansions

Pros: The story is amazing... Cons: it was developed by humans, and it is full of humans, therefore, be very certain, there will be flaws, both in the structure, and those who occupy it, though if you are reading this, you're most likely a human, so you'll just obliviously add to that flawed environment, without noticing the first nuance that makes it so, at which point, you will feel at home, and might consider the game perfectly satisfactory... If your humanity didn't make of you, first and foremost, a toxic and destructive being towards everything that is not yourself, that is... (Though i have left this review here, it is true for every single video game, except for those who don't have an amazing story, or a multiplayer environment.) (Also, mayhaps a handful of human beings, may have been unfairly classified as beings, toxic and destructive towards everything that is not themselves -those include human beings that are also destructive towards themselves -and, much more rarely, those who actually are constructive and delightful souls... To those, more specifically, the latter, i am sorry.) But yes, the main story, is without pair, if you are subjectively drawn into its themes and characters... The rest is rather primitive... A lot of rng in many systems, which bloats and wastes time, shallow systems, that only get deeper dozens of hours through each expansion, inconsistent side-quest designs, that fall far bellow the thoughtfulness of the main story, a poor excuse for a customisation system, and worst of all, a community that will defend all of those things, as human beings are want to, at every single place they might attribute to an extension of their personal validation... Incompetent people are due to defend incompetence... It was not a ironic review, ultimately...

Great Content!
Great Community and great support desk
The story is fantastic, the single character job switch is wonderful, and the community is very welcoming and helpful

very engaging game. ticks all my boxes. I've paid for basically a year sub (6 month x2) but took a long hiatus in the first 6 months between work odd shifts and just being exhausted, haven't had the pull to log in. however, now that I've got my schedule only set to certain days I plan to sink a lot more time into this game. if you like mmo's and have thought about getting this game, check out the free trial version. Base game plus 2 expansions. So there's literally no rush to get a subscription

To be honest, at first the game was not something that I wanted to play.
When I started I actually quit for a few months, due to the early game just being really slow and monotonous, which I genuinely think should be looked into.

Yeah the game does get better when you reach Heavensward but it is a bit daunting to have to wait till that part to actually say that the game ''gets better''

Of course some might enjoy the early game, but I for sure didn't, if you are someone who does not like slower type of combat and also progression, you might quit as well, the good news is that the free trial is now till Shadowbringers which, honestly was a very nice move on their end!

Even though I did not enjoy the early game I cannot deny how much this game has consumed me, it has been very fun to experience, especially Shadowbringers.

Gathering mounts, doing dungeons, raids, ect.

I do hope that they are able to make the main story able to be experienced with an actually friend of yours, since it was pretty annoying that me and my friend had to constantly make and disband a party while we were still in the beginning,

Overall, I would say that I really like this game! It has become some of my favorite out there, and also hearing what they have planned for Evercold, feels like it might be even better soon enough!

If you think that you can accept that the early game will be slower for you, for some time, then I would highly suggest that you give this game a go! You might just fall in love with it, like how I did.

Story is mediocore despite what the redditors and "that" community will have you believe, however if you don't care about that like i don't, this game offers a lot to do, the beginning is a bit of a slog and you cant really play with others until like level 15, but once you get there it picks up fast, i wasted like 10 hours leveling professions, and leveling every class i wanted to, a big plus is everything can be done with a single character and you can quick swap roles just by equipping a different weapon

Fun game to play solo or with friends. Challenging content if you're up for it but also a lot casual crafter/gatherer stuff. A lot of improvements to wardrobe/armoire so pieces are no longer job locked (as it should be). Overall a fun experience with a great story and lots to do.

One of the great mmo's. Fantastic class system where one character can swap between every class. Crafting is unique and much more in depth than any other game i've played if you choose to dedicate the time to level it. If you love Final Fantasy and want to play in a world full of other people, you must give this one a try! The free trial takes you extremely far into the game and does not limit you nearly as much as most others do. What are you waiting for Kupo!?
Just spent 60 hours thinking I wouldn't care much for the story, ended up actually enjoying A Realm Reborn and its story towards the end.  Cant wait to get to the other expansions :D

10/10

Plays well on nearly any hardware.  Friendly, engaging game, huge variety of content - casual play can take lliterelly years to play through the entire game.  Fun to re-play as well, easy to level multiple classes. Honestly one of the best games I've played over the years.
Early game review, so far fun and engaging. Keeps you drawn in if you like a good story
This game is beautiful, Vibrant and I would recommend it to anyone looking for a mmo to give a try.

Great MMO with a pretty slow and mundane Main story. However it gets better and better as you progress. Class system is great, endgame fashion looks amazing. Dungeons are fun to romp through as a tank.
The game is free to play until you sub, once you sub and the gametime runs out you cant play at all.

I don't know why I still hadn't done a review on this game. I've been playing it for 5 years now. Yes, Dawntrail sucked, but that's over with now, Evercold is coming with a lot of good changes and the story is already getting great again in the last patches. The gameplay is still really fun and the soundtrack is still awesome. The game has some of the greatest Final Fantasy stories ever told in Heavensward and Shadowbringers, a tale of war that gets better and more epic as it goes along in Stormblood, and a satisfying finale to the first saga in Endwalker. FFXIV quickly became, and still is, one of my favorite RPGs of all time.

Much like XI, this is an MMO designed like a mainline Final Fantasy title, with everything you'd expect from both sides of the coin. However, there are a few things that make this unique compared to other MMOs.

Since it's a mainline Final Fantasy game, the emphasis is put more on the journey and less on the destination. As a result, if you're a new player, you will be playing through the expansions as they came out whereas other modern MMOs make it their goal to rush you to the end as fast as possible. Progress is gated by your progress through the main story, which is arguably the largest focus of the game and how you will be spending most of your time. 

I've just gotten to Heavensward, and as a new player I've found this approach very refreshing. Too often MMO players treat their game less like a game and more like a chore, and it's promoted by the developers of these games agreeing and finding ways to remove content or invalidate things that have been done in the past. Of course XIV is partially guilty of this too, but not to the extent World of Warcraft is. It's nice to be able to take my time and experience content as I go, and unlocking brand new expansions feels very rewarding because of it. 

You will always find players for the main story (and in many cases it's not even needed since you have the options of NPCs in the story dungeons) due to the roulettes in the game, which also ensures that dungeons even released way back at launch sees activity in the present day. And if you're looking for classic MMO grinds, never fear, there's tons of content to hold you over throughout your journey. And for me, the best part of a lot of these is they also reward you with more worldbuilding, story, and lore. For example, each rep grind and each class/job in A Realm Reborn has their own story questline related to it. Optional dungeons also directly or indirectly tie into events or concepts touched on in the main story questline. 

If a heavily story-driven MMO is what you're itching for, this is what you're looking for.

Honestamente es uno de los mejores juegos que he jugado, la verdad lo recomiendo mucho, al princicio es un poco lento pero despues el juego te atrapa 100% pero deberas invertirle un poco de tiempo es un 10/10 muy buen juego.

While A Realm Reborn makes this game difficult to recommend due to its long cutscenes and hour wall (ARR is over 200 quests long compared to every other expansion!), the game makes up for it in every other aspect. A strong set of customization features with more on the way, the community are some of the friendliest people on the planet and it's a great way to relax and make some friends. I am currently in Endwalker at the time of writing this and Shadowbringers (Which will be in the free trial soon!) is hands down peak final fantasy for this game. Definitely recommend it if you have lots of time to fully enjoy the game!

I'm not an MMO fan. I've really tried to get into it over the years and even the base game of XIV took me a while to get into. The second I was done with it though, I was so drawn that I didn't put the game down once until I beat it. When I say that the story alone is worth the time spent, I really mean it. This game is special. It probably always will be to me
Friendly community. Good story. Even normal content has interesting fight mechanics.
I'm told I'm almost at the part where the game starts getting fun.

Great character customization, story, map disign, multiplayer, and combat, Though it should be pointed out like any MMO, the game starts a bit slow with the game filling you in on the plotline in different ways and quests being basic and not that rewarding to complete. Still a great game for people looking for a adictive MMO to burn long hours into.
Do you love not having a life and playing the same game over and over again? WELL I HAVE THE GAME FOR YOU

I came back to this game after a 6-7 (lmaooo) month break and had no issues hopping back in.
Managed to get my money's worth in playtime out of the game so far, and I'm just now getting close to the end of Heavensward. I still have like 500+ hours of story content to go through, and I'm looking forward to it.

As someone who only plays tanks in MMOs and most games, this game really has a satisfying job/role system where you feel the distinction between each role.

Don't let the catgirls and gooners throw you away from this game. I just do my dungeons and story content and still have fun with the game.

Probably the best MMO on the market right now (waiting to see the Riot MMO), especially if you are a fan of final fantasy in general. The only things the game lacks is actually relevant dialogue options in the MSQ and better gearing (at least where I am right now). What I mean is that gearing in the game is generally very linear where you just straight up have stats sticks instead of the ability to make builds yourself but I guess it's just not that kind of game. But yea all in all the game is very good and the free trial is huge now so you should def at least give it a try. Sorry if this is worded poorly :D.

I've been playing MMOs for ever and trust me when I say this one is a good one. Engaging story, pretty easy to pick up but is a good challenge to complete. The progression is satisfying and rewarding. You can also do everything on the same character.

Biggest point : THE GLAM GAME ! One of the best skin design gameplay with tons of possibilities !!

Really enjoy this game, hope you do too :)
Played for hundreds of hours on the PlayStation. Happy to buy it again on Steam to play on the PC, and especially the Steam Deck.

Fun game with amazing story. There are a lot of ups and downs with this game as with all MMOs but this is a story driven game with MMO aspects stapled on. You can play for the story or the community or both, but be aware the game starts slow and builds up. I found that treating the story as a visual novel with better gameplay between story beats to help me with the early section which can turn off a lot of players.
Good game great game even better story but please steam stop asking for a review.

Lots of fun to try out new combat classes and find out what feels the most fun. Also interesting crafting and gathering classes. You still need a combat class, but you can put plenty of time into crafting and gathering.

At the time of writing this review, I’ve just finished A Realm Reborn and am about to start Heavensward.
I think I’ll keep the review updated every time I finish an expansion.
ARR isn’t bad at all, well, apart from the first quarter of the game, which is dreadfully long and tedious, but you’ve got to lay the foundations for a world emerging from a cataclysm (aka a disastrous 1.0).
I really love the fact that the reboot is integrated into the lore and not just ignored, as if it had never existed.
A list of pros and cons will be simpler.
For context, I played as Hrothgar  Warrior all the way through.

Pros:
- Very good voice acting (I’m playing in French)
- A story that takes its time to set the scene, but once it gets going, it’s full steam ahead
- Likeable characters you can really get attached to
- Pretty cool creature and armour designs
- The option to have your chocobo fight alongside you (handy to have a pocket healer)

The cons:
-The Lalafell, these creatures shouldn't exist; it's impossible to feel the slightest bit of sympathy for these repulsive beings; every time I saw one, I was taken out of the story, except for Papalimo and Tataru.
-The first quarter of the game is sooo long.
-The extremes that aren't explained at all; you're given tutorials throughout the game, but then suddenly nothing, and if you want to understand a single thing, go read the wikis or get told to ♥♥♥♥ off by your teammates
-Almost no helmets for my race

Right then, off to Ishgard!

Would hold heartly recommend this game, comparing this to any other MMO out there, this by far tops them all, story, ""don't get me started on that part, french kiss"" and tons of things to do, and with the constant update of new content big win!!

11/10 Score

Have you heard of the critically acclaimed Final Fantasy 14 Online with a now expanded free trial where you can now play Shadowbringers and go all the way up to lvl 80 without limit! Oh wow! Amazing!

Just reached Dawntrail on my adventure with nearly 500 hours in the game already, so much peak content and I cannot wait for Evercold to release on January
If you can get past the narrative and gameplay nonsense that often mires MMOs, you will experience true peak.

DO NOT buy the steam version. The keys are often not recognized (like mine) and youll be denied service. Buy it directly from SE website, otherwise theres a chance you end up like me with a key the game wont recognize
cant log in launcher wont work, cant download update error codes why is it so difficult to play this game anyone have a work around let me know.

So, it is a fun traditional mmorpg experience. Pros and cons to follow

pros: 
-cool jobs, especially if you are an FF fan
-all jobs are same character, so you don't have to reroll
-graphics are crisp and clean and still look good
-I personally like the mob aesthetic
-Easy system for pugging, harder if you are dps, since it is traditional holy trinity group with tank/healer/x2 dps
- Crafting is well developed as is gathering
- One of the least predatory models, you pay a sub, you get the game, you wanna pay more sub you get extra space, you already have a decent amount of space
- Community is friendly to casuals and new players
- Can play a lot of the dungeons with NPCs and they do quite well
- Great writing and interesting plot, I'm too ADD to care, but it is quite good and even got my attention at moments.

Cons:
- Too many buttons, like little things that should just be passive auras imo are active skills, this is getting streamlined, so should change later 2026
- I personally find the boss and raid mechanics to be a little too much like dance revolution, where perfect choreography gets compounded with the sheer number of buttons and makes pugging frustrating and to be honest, I have trouble keeping up with. I end up queuing as DPS more than healer or tank because it gives me anxiety. I find myself playing with the NPCs as much as possible because of this.
- Like any large and established mmo, you are gonna be googling a lot of systems to play the game, some systems just feel like old and worse versions of newer ones.
- Despite a pretty fair subscription model, storage bloat is a real problem, especially if you craft, which leads to a lot of time micromanaging space vs other activities.
- There is a new player mentor system that relies on mentors to act in a very semi-official gm-lite capacity in that chat and they aren't on the same page with each other and what is and isn't appropriate chatting. This leads to what I perceive as a bit of gatekeeping from some individuals.
- This is very subjective, but I think the the main quests have a lot of moments and quests that are just filler to take more time. I have seen a lot of quests to go find "things" in some big red circles on the map that are semi-hidden and amount to just dragging out the current step. But it's a subscription model and I'll take that over FTP.

All in all, there aren't a lot of good mmorpgs to even play out there compared to the shameless money grab pump-and-dump ones and the devs here care about their craft and their product. I'm an FF fan from FF4, I think it is a great game overall and the system revamp coming up is something I am looking forward to. I am quite happy with it.

Fun, but the dungeons are flawed for new players. Everyone is an older player, so if you get into dungeons, the vast majority of players have already done the content and rush through, totally decimating the fun. 
If I played the game when it came out and followed it through every expansion, I'm sure I could recommend it, but ARR is boring and bland and I was falling asleep. I had my fun, and the game is amazing with so much content, however beginners have to spend a solid 100+ hours doing the mind numbing main quest that feels so worthless.

Can't even log in after too many security checks and apparently wrong emails/passwords, overly secure just for a first time attempt to play, offer these things after managing to actually get in the game.

sick game. but don't tell anyone that you skip all the cut scenes and just slam the quests as quick as possible. lol these people are genuinely unhinged when it comes to that stuff. like aye bro nobody is stoppin you from playin the game how you want. lol but friendly players outside of that. oh and shoutout to this one person who gave me the whale minion after randomly interacting with them in town. that was cool

You can now enjoy the game for free up through Shadowbringers, the best expansion. Paying for post-Endwalker is paying for everything you loved about XIV to be ruined. The free experience will deliver you a rich, compelling, immersive story that will make the game ascend the sum of its parts. Endwalker cleanly wraps it up in a nice little package. Then Dawntrail will ♥♥♥♥ on that package, brutalize your immersion and leave you traumatized.

Just enjoy this as a free game. At this rate even Endwalker will be free at some point.
